mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity investors to price in lower future earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in durable goods and residential investment, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and pushes S&P 500 forward returns positive over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, indicating that supply growth is being fully offset by demand destruction.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, leading to higher forward equity returns over a 60-day horizon as fundamental valuation metrics adjust to stronger macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 5-year period, or forward returns show no significant premium during GDP acceleration phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index above 0.4% forces market participants to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which increases the equity risk premium and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for > 65% of instances following CPI spikes above the threshold, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate return forecast model, or forward returns are not significantly positive when payrolls rise > 150k and unemployment falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 800 thousand units alongside rising consumer credit balances signal a broad-based credit-fueled consumption boom, which lifts cyclically sensitive equity sectors and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative when both housing starts and consumer credit exceed their respective thresholds, indicating that credit expansion is being driven by deleveraging or non-consumption sectors.
